name: Dran Sword 3-60F
assessment: The first Beyblade X starter ever released. Dran Sword 3-60F is a right-spin Attack type built around aggressive Xtreme Dash strikes. Its three-sided Blade focuses all force on a few contact points, while the Flat Bit provides the speed and grip needed to climb the X-Line repeatedly. A strong introductory combo for aggressive playstyles.

name: Hells Scythe 4-60T
assessment: Hells Scythe 4-60T is marketed as a Balance type, but its real strength lies in raw Stamina. The four-sided Blade has excellent spin retention, though the gaps between the blades create noticeable recoil that can make battles unpredictable. The Taper Bit adds some mobility, making it a decent all-rounder that leans Stamina.

name: Knight Shield 3-80N
assessment: Knight Shield 3-80N is a right-spin Defense type designed to absorb impacts rather than dodge them. Its six protective blades form a shock-absorbing structure, while the Needle Bit keeps the Bey centered in the stadium. A reliable defensive stock combo that performs consistently against mixed opponents.

name: Wizard Arrow 4-80B
assessment: Wizard Arrow 4-80B is a right-spin Stamina type and the dedicated Stamina starter of the first wave. Its rounded Blade and Ball Bit reduce friction and keep the Bey stable in the center of the stadium. A straightforward choice for outlasting opponents rather than knocking them out.

name: Knight Lance 4-80HN
assessment: Knight Lance 4-80HN is a right-spin Defense type released as a Booster in mid-2023. It combines the taller Knight Lance Blade with the 4-80 Ratchet and High Needle Bit, sitting high in the stadium to deflect incoming attacks. Often described as a versatile 'counter' combo that trades pure stamina for survivability.

name: Shark Edge 3-60LF
assessment: Shark Edge 3-60LF is a right-spin Attack type built around two large, aggressive blades. The sloped shape generates strong Smash and Upper Attack, while the Low Flat Bit keeps the combo low and fast for repeated X-Line charges. A hard-hitting choice that sacrifices some stamina for knockout power.

name: LeonClaw 5-60P
assessment: LeonClaw 5-60P is a right-spin Attack type released in late 2023. Its defining feature is the new 5-60 Ratchet paired with the Point Bit, giving the combo a sharp, aggressive movement pattern. The Leon Claw Blade delivers focused strikes, making it a solid pick for bladers who want speed and burst potential.

name: DranDagger 4-60R
assessment: DranDagger 4-60R is a right-spin Attack type released as part of the Dran Dagger Deck Set. It is considered just as viable as the original Dran Sword 3-60F, with the Rush Bit adding extra speed and the 4-60 Ratchet keeping the height moderate. A strong successor for aggressive right-spin decks.

name: HellsChain 5-60HT
assessment: HellsChain 5-60HT is a right-spin Balance type that acts as a more endurance-focused variant of Hells Scythe. The Chain-shaped Blade still carries attack potential, but the High Taper Bit and 5-60 Ratchet give it better stability and longer spin times than the original stock combo.

name: RhinoHorn 3-80S
assessment: RhinoHorn 3-80S is a right-spin combo valued for its surprising stamina and defensive resilience. The Rhino Horn Blade's bulky shape pairs well with the tall 3-80 Ratchet and Spike Bit, making it a durable choice that can survive long exchanges while still delivering steady counter-hits.

name: PhoenixFeather 3-60F
assessment: PhoenixFeather 3-60F is a right-spin Attack type and a limited-release prize Bey. Its three upward-slanting wings create fast, aggressive contact points similar to Dran Sword, while the 3-60 Ratchet and Flat Bit keep the combo low and dash-friendly. A collectible but battle-capible attack option.

name: WyvernGale 2-60S
assessment: WyvernGale 2-60S is a right-spin combo built around a smooth, low-recoil Blade. While it lacks the weight of some later releases, its shape gives it solid stamina potential, and the 2-60 Ratchet with Spike Bit keeps the combo stable in the center. A reliable stamina-oriented stock combo.

name: UnicornSting 5-60GP
assessment: UnicornSting 5-60GP is a right-spin Balance type released in early 2024. Its asymmetrical Blade mixes attack and defense blades, while the Gear Point Bit can switch between aggression and stability depending on launch. A versatile combo that adapts its role mid-battle.

name: SphinxCowl 4-80HT
assessment: SphinxCowl 4-80HT is a right-spin combo from the BX-27 Random Booster lineup. Its layered, defensive-looking Blade works well with the tall 4-80 Ratchet and High Taper Bit, offering a mix of endurance and height-based defense. A visually distinctive combo with solid all-around performance.

name: ViperTail 5-80O
assessment: ViperTail 5-80O is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Takara Tomy, released as BX-16. Released on 07.10.2023. Parts: Viper Tail / 5-80 / Orb.

name: Sword Dran 3-60F
assessment: Sword Dran 3-60F is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as F9580. Parts: Dran Sword / 3-60 / Flat.

name: Helm Knight 3-80N
assessment: Helm Knight 3-80N is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as F9581. Parts: Knight Shield / 3-80 / Needle.

name: Arrow Wizard 4-80B
assessment: Arrow Wizard 4-80B is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as F9582. Parts: Wizard Arrow / 4-80 / Ball.

name: Scythe Incendio 4-60T
assessment: Scythe Incendio 4-60T is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as F9583. Parts: Hells Scythe / 4-60 / Taper.

name: Steel Samurai 4-80T
assessment: Steel Samurai 4-80T is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as G0188. Parts: Samurai Steel / 4-80 / Taper.

name: Horn Rhino 3-80S
assessment: Horn Rhino 3-80S is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as G0192. Parts: Rhino Horn / 3-80 / Spike.

name: Keel Shark 3-60LF
assessment: Keel Shark 3-60LF is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as G0194. Parts: Shark Edge / 3-60 / Low Flat.

name: Talon Ptera 3-80B
assessment: Talon Ptera 3-80B is a right-spin factory-combined Beyblade from Hasbro, released as G0195. Parts: Ptera Swing / 3-80 / Ball.

name: WizardArrow 4-80B
assessment: A red recolor of the WizardArrow starter, released as a Booster. The Stamina-focused Blade and Ball Bit combination remains identical in performance to the yellow BX-03 version, offering a lower-cost entry point for the same stamina archetype.

name: KnightShield 3-80N
assessment: A blue recolor of KnightShield released as a Booster. It keeps the same defensive six-bladed shape and Needle Bit, making it a solid budget option for players who want a second defensive combo without buying the starter again.

name: HellsScythe 3-80B
assessment: Exclusive to the BX-08 3on3 Deck Set. Pairing the stamina-heavy HellsScythe Blade with the tall 3-80 Ratchet and Ball Bit creates a stamina combo built to outlast opponents in longer exchanges, though it remains vulnerable to strong attackers.

name: WizardArrow 4-60N
assessment: Exclusive to the BX-08 3on3 Deck Set. By lowering the height with the 4-60 Ratchet and adding the Needle Bit, this WizardArrow variant leans into defense and counter-attack rather than pure stamina, giving the deck a rounded profile.

name: KnightShield 4-80T
assessment: Exclusive to the BX-08 3on3 Deck Set. The taller 4-80 Ratchet and Taper Bit give this KnightShield variant more mobility than the stock combo, turning it into a defensive-balance hybrid that can still absorb hits while moving around the stadium.

name: SharkEdge 3-60LF
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 1. This low-profile SharkEdge combo is built to dive under opponents and attack their Ratchet directly. The 3-60 Ratchet and Low Flat Bit keep it fast and close to the stadium floor for aggressive dive attacks.

name: SharkEdge 4-80N
assessment: A yellow recolor from Random Booster Vol. 1. The Needle Bit and taller 4-80 Ratchet give this SharkEdge variant a more stamina-oriented behavior, trading some aggression for better survival in the center of the stadium.

name: DranSword 3-80B
assessment: A black recolor from Random Booster Vol. 1. Mounting the DranSword Blade on a tall 3-80 Ratchet with a Ball Bit creates an unusual stamina-leaning Attack combo that can still deliver hits while outlasting lighter opponents.

name: HellsScythe 4-80LF
assessment: A green recolor from Random Booster Vol. 1. Combining HellsScythe's high stamina with the aggressive Low Flat Bit turns this into a mobile balance combo that can dash along the X-Line while retaining decent spin time.

name: KnightShield 4-60LF
assessment: A cyan recolor from Random Booster Vol. 1. The low 4-60 Ratchet and Low Flat Bit are an unusual pairing for the defensive KnightShield Blade, producing a lower, more mobile defensive variant that can still absorb impacts.

name: WizardArrow 3-60T
assessment: A brown recolor from Random Booster Vol. 1. The Taper Bit and 3-60 Ratchet give this WizardArrow variant more movement than the stock Ball combo, leaning into a balance playstyle that can both dodge and outlast.

name: ViperTail 4-60F
assessment: A black recolor from Random Booster ViperTail Select. The Flat Bit turns the normally stamina-focused ViperTail into an aggressive dash combo, using its downward-sloping blades to chase opponents around the stadium.

name: ViperTail 3-80HN
assessment: A yellow recolor from Random Booster ViperTail Select. The High Needle Bit and tall 3-80 Ratchet push this ViperTail variant toward pure stamina and survival, keeping it centered and stable throughout long battles.

name: DranSword 3-60F
assessment: A red recolor of DranSword included in the BX-17 Battle Entry Set. Performance matches the blue BX-01 starter, offering the same aggressive three-bladed Attack combo for players buying the entry bundle.

name: WizardArrow 4-80B
assessment: A blue recolor of WizardArrow included in the BX-17 Battle Entry Set. It keeps the same stamina-oriented Blade and Ball Bit as the yellow starter, providing a defensive Stamina option in the bundle.

name: DranSword 3-60F
assessment: The BX-22 Entry Package repackages the original DranSword 3-60F with a compact entry launcher at a lower price point. Ideal as a backup or gift set without changing the proven Attack combo.

name: PhoenixWing 9-60GF
assessment: PhoenixWing 9-60GF is a right-spin Attack type and a major meta-defining release. Its heavy three-bladed Blade features protruding Launcher Hooks that align with the 9-60 Ratchet to create powerful Attack Points, while Gear Flat delivers extreme Xtreme Dash speed. One of the strongest Attack combos in the early metagame.

name: WyvernGale 5-80GB
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 2. WyvernGale 5-80GB pairs the smooth, low-recoil WyvernGale Blade with the wide Gear Ball Bit for a stamina combo that can absorb hits and counter-attack with its aerodynamic shape.

name: WyvernGale 3-60T
assessment: A recolor from Random Booster Vol. 2. The 3-60 Ratchet and Taper Bit give this WyvernGale variant more controlled movement, leaning toward a balance playstyle that can still outlast aggressive opponents.

name: KnightLance 4-60GB
assessment: A black recolor from Random Booster Vol. 2. Combining KnightLance's high defensive stance with the Gear Ball Bit creates a durable combo that stays centered and survives repeated collisions.

name: ViperTail 5-60F
assessment: A recolor from Random Booster Vol. 2. The Flat Bit turns ViperTail into a mobile attacker, using its downward-sloping blades to chase and whittle opponents rather than camping the center.

name: LeonClaw 3-80HN
assessment: A recolor from Random Booster Vol. 2. The tall 3-80 Ratchet and High Needle Bit give LeonClaw a higher center of gravity and better endurance, shifting it toward a defensive-balance role.

name: WizardArrow 4-80GB
assessment: A recolor from Random Booster Vol. 2. The Gear Ball Bit gives WizardArrow improved stamina and KO resistance compared to the standard Ball Bit, making this one of the better stamina variants of the Blade.

name: TyrannoBeat 4-70Q
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 3. TyrannoBeat 4-70Q is a right-spin Attack type built around the heavy Tyranno Beat Blade and the erratic Quake Bit. The 4-70 Ratchet keeps the combo at medium height, while Quake's elliptical tip produces unpredictable jumps that can catch opponents off guard. A high-risk, high-reward aggressive combo.

name: TyrannoBeat 3-60S
assessment: A secondary pull from Random Booster Vol. 3. TyrannoBeat 3-60S swaps the Quake Bit for Spike, giving the aggressive Blade more controlled endurance. The 3-60 Ratchet sits lower than 4-70, creating a stamina-leaning Attack combo that can still deliver strong hits.

name: HellsChain 9-80O
assessment: A stamina-oriented pull from Random Booster Vol. 3. HellsChain 9-80O pairs the chain-shaped Blade with the tall 9-80 Ratchet and Orb Bit, emphasizing spin retention and stability in the center of the stadium. Less aggressive than other Vol. 3 combos, but more consistent in longer battles.

name: DranDagger 4-70P
assessment: An Attack pull from Random Booster Vol. 3. DranDagger 4-70P combines the six-bladed Dran Dagger Blade with the versatile Point Bit. Depending on launch angle it can either rush the X-Line aggressively or camp the center for stamina, giving it a balance-attack hybrid playstyle.

name: SharkEdge 1-60Q
assessment: An aggressive pull from Random Booster Vol. 3. SharkEdge 1-60Q uses the imbalanced 1-60 Ratchet together with the Quake Bit, creating an extremely mobile and unpredictable attacker. The heavy Shark Edge Blade provides Smash and Upper Attack, but the combo burns stamina quickly.

name: RhinoHorn 5-80Q
assessment: A bulky pull from Random Booster Vol. 3. RhinoHorn 5-80Q pairs the wide Rhino Horn Blade with the tall 5-80 Ratchet and Quake Bit. The result is a heavier, less agile attacker that relies on inertia and surprise jumps rather than pure speed.

name: WeissTiger 3-60U
assessment: WeissTiger 3-60U is a right-spin Balance type released as a Booster in mid-2024. Its Blade mixes Upper, Damper, and Smash blades, while the Unite Bit behaves similarly to Taper with a wider ball shape. A versatile Team Pendragon release that can shift between aggression and stability depending on launch.

name: CobaltDragoon 2-60C
assessment: CobaltDragoon 2-60C is the first left-spin Beyblade in the Beyblade X series and a direct homage to the original Dragoon. The four upward-slanting blades generate powerful Attack, while the Cyclone Bit enables fast, aggressive movement. A landmark release for left-spin Attack decks.

name: BlackShell 4-60D
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 4. BlackShell 4-60D is a right-spin Defense type built around the new Black Shell Blade. The low 4-60 Ratchet and Dot Bit keep the combo close to the stadium floor, while the downward-facing defensive blades are designed to deflect attacks from below.

name: BlackShell 9-80B
assessment: A secondary pull from Random Booster Vol. 4. BlackShell 9-80B raises the height with the 9-80 Ratchet and uses the Ball Bit for centering, turning the defensive Blade into a stamina-oriented wall that can outlast aggressive opponents.

name: UnicornSting 3-70D
assessment: A pull from Random Booster Vol. 4. UnicornSting 3-70D combines the asymmetrical Unicorn Sting Blade with the mid-height 3-70 Ratchet and Dot Bit. The result is a balance-defense hybrid that stays grounded and counters with its mixed blade shapes.

name: WizardRod 1-60R
assessment: A pull from Random Booster Vol. 4. WizardRod 1-60R mounts the round Wizard Rod Blade on the imbalanced 1-60 Ratchet with the Rush Bit, creating a fast, mobile combo that can dash into opponents and retreat quickly.

name: PhoenixWing 5-80H
assessment: A pull from Random Booster Vol. 4. PhoenixWing 5-80H pairs the heavy Phoenix Wing Blade with the tall 5-80 Ratchet and Hexa Bit. The low-profile Hexa tip keeps the combo stable while the wide Ratchet and heavy Blade provide strong Smash Attack potential.

name: ViperTail 5-70D
assessment: A pull from Random Booster Vol. 4. ViperTail 5-70D uses the mid-height 5-70 Ratchet and Dot Bit with the downward-sloping Viper Tail Blade. This variant leans into a defensive-balance role, absorbing hits while maintaining enough stamina to outlast rushdown combos.

name: WhaleWave 5-80E
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster WhaleWave Select. WhaleWave 5-80E is a right-spin Balance type built around the new Whale Wave Blade. The Elevate Bit can lift the tip during Xtreme Dash for overhead smash attacks, while the 5-80 Ratchet provides a tall, stable platform.

name: WhaleWave 4-70HN
assessment: A secondary pull from Random Booster WhaleWave Select. WhaleWave 4-70HN is a right-spin Defense type that uses the 4-70 Ratchet and High Needle Bit to keep the bulky Whale Wave Blade centered and resilient against attackers.

name: WhaleWave 3-80GB
assessment: A pull from Random Booster WhaleWave Select. WhaleWave 3-80GB is a right-spin Stamina type that pairs the smooth Whale Wave Blade with the tall 3-80 Ratchet and Gear Ball Bit, prioritizing long spin times and KO resistance.

name: CrimsonGaruda 4-70TP
assessment: CrimsonGaruda 4-70TP is a right-spin Balance type released as a Booster in late 2024. Its Blade performs differently on top and bottom edges, and the Trans Point Bit can switch between High and Low modes during battle. A versatile combo that adapts to opponents of various heights.

name: ShelterDrake 7-80GP
assessment: The prize Bey of Random Booster ShelterDrake Select. ShelterDrake 7-80GP is a right-spin Balance type and a Team Pendragon homage. The oval-shaped, two-sided Blade focuses on parrying and durability, while the new 7-80 Ratchet and Gear Point Bit provide a wide, stable base with switchable aggression.

name: ShelterDrake 5-70O
assessment: A secondary pull from Random Booster ShelterDrake Select. ShelterDrake 5-70O uses the 5-70 Ratchet and Orb Bit to keep the Shelter Drake Blade centered and stable, leaning into a defensive-stamina playstyle.

name: ShelterDrake 3-60D
assessment: A pull from Random Booster ShelterDrake Select. ShelterDrake 3-60D lowers the height with the 3-60 Ratchet and Dot Bit, turning the Shelter Drake Blade into a grounded defensive combo that can withstand low attackers.

name: TriceraPress M-85BS
assessment: TriceraPress M-85BS is a right-spin Defense type released as a Booster in mid-2025. It introduces the M-85 Ratchet, the heaviest Ratchet in the Basic Line thanks to its metal ring, paired with the Bound Spike Bit. The Tricera Press Blade's launcher hooks double as contact points, creating a tall, heavy combo that controls space and survives knockbacks.

name: SamuraiCalibur 6-70M
assessment: SamuraiCalibur 6-70M is a right-spin Balance type released as a Booster in 2025. It uses the new Samurai Calibur Blade with small and large blades for early-game rushes and late-game heavy strikes. The Merge Bit's rubber provides extra grip when tilted, while the 6-70 Ratchet offers a balanced height profile.

name: DranStrike 4-50FF
assessment: DranStrike 4-50FF is a right-spin Attack type starter released in 2026. It debuts the Basic Line Expand Blade gimmick, where metal extends through the Gear Chip area to increase rigidity and weight. The low 4-50 Ratchet and Free Flat Bit enable fast, repeated attacks.

name: CobaltDrake 4-60F
assessment: CobaltDrake 4-60F is a right-spin Attack type Rare Bey Get Battle prize and the first Team Pendragon release. At launch it was the heaviest Blade in the game, with four upward-slanting contact points similar to a smaller Dran Sword. A highly collectible early-meta Attack combo.

name: DranzerSpiral 3-80T
assessment: DranzerSpiral 3-80T is a right-spin Balance type X-Over Project remake of the original Dranzer S. Its two long blades enable Upper Attack, but the Blade is notably light at under 28 grams. The Taper Bit and 3-80 Ratchet give it mobile balance characteristics, making it a nostalgic but fragile choice.

name: Lightning L-Drago 1-60F (Upper Type)
assessment: The Upper Type prize from the Lightning L-Drago Random Booster. This left-spin Attack type features three upward-sloping blades designed for Upper Attack, continuing the L-Drago legacy in the X Series. The 1-60 Ratchet and Flat Bit keep the combo low and aggressive.

name: Lightning L-Drago 1-60F (Rapid Hit Type)
assessment: The Rapid Hit Type pull from the Lightning L-Drago Random Booster. This left-spin Attack type uses three continuous hit blades for rush-down combos. Like the Upper Type, it runs on the 1-60 Ratchet and Flat Bit for speed and Xtreme Dash potential.

name: Dragoon Storm 4-60RA
assessment: Dragoon Storm 4-60RA is a left-spin Attack type included in the Beyblade 25th Anniversary Set. It is an X Series remake of Tyson's original Dragoon Storm, using the Rubber Accel Bit for extreme speed and the 4-60 Ratchet for a standard height profile.

name: Storm Pegasis 3-70RA
assessment: Storm Pegasis 3-70RA is a right-spin Attack type included in the Beyblade 25th Anniversary Set. It remakes the Metal Fight Storm Pegasis with three aggressive wings and the Rubber Accel Bit for high-speed Xtreme Dash attacks.

name: Victory Valkyrie 2-60RA
assessment: Victory Valkyrie 2-60RA is a right-spin Attack type included in the Beyblade 25th Anniversary Set. It brings the Burst-era Victory Valkyrie into the X Series with its iconic winged shape and the Rubber Accel Bit for aggressive movement.

name: DranBuster 1-60A
assessment: DranBuster 1-60A is the first Unique Line starter and a right-spin Attack type. Its wide impact-focused Blade, 1-60 Ratchet and Accel Bit are tuned for aggressive Xtreme Dash strikes, while the resin launcher hooks push weight outward for a distinct feel from the BX Dran Sword.

name: HellsHammer 3-70H
assessment: HellsHammer 3-70H is a right-spin Attack type Unique Line starter. The heavy HellsHammer Blade combines with the new 3-70 Ratchet and the fast Hexa Bit to produce strong smash attack and rapid X-Dash acceleration.

name: WizardRod 5-70DB
assessment: WizardRod 5-70DB is a right-spin Stamina type Unique Line Booster built around centrifugal force. Its wide circular Blade and Disk Ball Bit keep the combo centered and stable, making it one of the dominant Stamina options of the UX line.

name: DranBuster 1-60A (Special Ver.)
assessment: DranBuster 1-60A (Special Ver.) is the Bey included in the Battle Entry Set U. It uses the same parts as the UX-01 release in a special color scheme, offering the same aggressive Attack performance with a different look.

name: ShinobiShadow 9-60LF
assessment: ShinobiShadow 9-60LF is a right-spin Defense type released in Random Booster ShinobiShadow Select. Its compact three-sided Blade and narrow 9-60 Ratchet reduce burst exposure, while the Low Flat Bit adds enough mobility to contest the X-Line.

name: LeonCrest 7-60GN
assessment: LeonCrest 7-60GN is a right-spin Defense type Unique Line Booster. Its thick outer wings and Gear Needle Bit make it a grounded, counter-heavy combo that absorbs hits and stays upright.

name: PhoenixRudder 9-70G
assessment: PhoenixRudder 9-70G is a right-spin Stamina type included in the PhoenixRudder Deck Set. It succeeds PhoenixWing, pairing the 9-70 Ratchet with the Glide Bit for high spin retention and stability.

name: SilverWolf 3-80FB
assessment: SilverWolf 3-80FB is a right-spin Stamina type Unique Line Starter and a homage to Wolborg. Its free-spinning ring is designed to deflect attacks, while the Free Ball Bit supports long spin times. Note that the Blade has seen notable breakage reports in competitive use.

name: SamuraiSaber 2-70L
assessment: SamuraiSaber 2-70L is a right-spin Attack type Unique Line Starter. Its gimmick lets the protrusions retract to switch between consecutive strikes and a single high-impact hit, while the Level Bit enables aggressive movement.

name: KnightMail 3-85BS
assessment: KnightMail 3-85BS is a right-spin Defense type included in the Customize Set U. Its tall 3-85 Ratchet and Bound Spike Bit are built around absorbing impacts and recovering stability, continuing the Knight lineage in the Unique Line.

name: ImpactDrake 9-60LR
assessment: ImpactDrake 9-60LR is a right-spin Attack type Unique Line Starter. It marks the return of rubber contact points to the Unique Line, combining the 9-60 Ratchet with the fast Low Rush Bit for hard-hitting, mobile attacks.

name: GhostCircle 0-80GB
assessment: GhostCircle 0-80GB is a right-spin Stamina type and the prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 5. Its 0-80 Ratchet has no protrusions, prioritizing low burst risk and high spin retention, while the Gear Ball Bit keeps the combo centered.

name: GhostCircle 4-60H
assessment: GhostCircle 4-60H is a right-spin Balance type variant from Random Booster Vol. 5. It pairs the GhostCircle Blade with the 4-60 Ratchet and Hexa Bit for a lower, more mobile setup than the prize version.

name: ShinobiShadow 3-80F
assessment: ShinobiShadow 3-80F is a right-spin Attack type variant from Random Booster Vol. 5. The taller 3-80 Ratchet and Flat Bit make it more aggressive and mobile than the original 9-60LF release.

name: LeonClaw 0-80E
assessment: LeonClaw 0-80E is a right-spin Balance type from Random Booster Vol. 5. The 0-80 Ratchet and new Elevate Bit give it a unique tilted-attack behavior.

name: PhoenixFeather 2-60N
assessment: PhoenixFeather 2-60N is a right-spin Stamina type from Random Booster Vol. 5. It uses the 2-60 Ratchet and Needle Bit to stay centered and outlast opponents.

name: WyvernGale 0-80C
assessment: WyvernGale 0-80C is a right-spin Attack type from Random Booster Vol. 5. The 0-80 Ratchet and Cyclone Bit create an aggressive, wide-movement combo.

name: GolemRock 1-60UN
assessment: GolemRock 1-60UN is a right-spin Defense type Unique Line Booster. Its bulky Blade and Unite Bit provide a stable, grounded presence.

name: ScorpioSpear 0-70Z
assessment: ScorpioSpear 0-70Z is a right-spin Balance type Starter. Its large, transformable Blade shifts between a defensive round shape and an attacking spear shape, while the Zap Bit offers versatile behavior depending on launch angle.

name: SharkScale 4-50UF
assessment: SharkScale 4-50UF is a right-spin Attack type included in the SharkScale Deck Set. Its two large contact points and the low 4-50 Ratchet pair with the new Under Flat Bit for aggressive low-height attacks.

name: TyrannoRoar 1-70L
assessment: TyrannoRoar 1-70L is a right-spin Balance type included in the SharkScale Deck Set. The 1-70 Ratchet and Level Bit support a versatile mix of offense and stability.

name: HellsBrave J3-60GF
assessment: HellsBrave J3-60GF is a right-spin Attack type included in the SharkScale Deck Set. It uses the Custom Line format with the Jaggy Assist Blade, 3-60 Ratchet and Gear Flat Bit for aggressive movement.

name: ClockMirage 9-65B (Prize)
assessment: ClockMirage 9-65B is a right-spin Stamina type and the prize Bey of Random Booster ClockMirage Select. Its 60-edge circular Blade, 9-65 Ratchet and Ball Bit maximize spin time.

name: ClockMirage 9-65B (Gray/White Ver.)
assessment: A color variant of ClockMirage 9-65B from Random Booster ClockMirage Select, using the same Stamina-focused parts.

name: ClockMirage 9-65B (Pink/Yellow Ver.)
assessment: A color variant of ClockMirage 9-65B from Random Booster ClockMirage Select, using the same Stamina-focused parts.

name: MeteorDragoon 3-70J
assessment: MeteorDragoon 3-70J is a right-spin Attack type Unique Line Starter. Its design emphasizes powerful strikes, using the 3-70 Ratchet and Jolt Bit for high mobility.

name: MummyCurse 7-55W
assessment: MummyCurse 7-55W is a right-spin Defense type and the prize Bey of Random Booster Vol. 8. Its bandage-wrapped Blade is built to absorb impacts, while the 7-55 Ratchet and Wedge Bit provide a low, stable center of gravity.

name: MummyCurse 4-60C
assessment: MummyCurse 4-60C is a right-spin Attack type variant from Random Booster Vol. 8. The 4-60 Ratchet and Cyclone Bit add more movement compared to the prize version.

name: PegasusBrush M3-85W
assessment: PegasusBrush M3-85W is a right-spin Stamina type from Random Booster Vol. 8. It uses the Custom Line format with the Massive Assist Blade, 3-85 Ratchet and Wedge Bit for height and survivability.

name: SolBrave C9-70TP
assessment: SolBrave C9-70TP is a right-spin Balance type from Random Booster Vol. 8. It uses the Custom Line format with the Charge Assist Blade, 9-70 Ratchet and Trans Point Bit for versatile movement.

name: DranDagger 7-55G
assessment: DranDagger 7-55G is a right-spin Attack type from Random Booster Vol. 8. The 7-55 Ratchet and Glide Bit create a low, aggressive combo.

name: WeissTiger 4-80LR
assessment: WeissTiger 4-80LR is a right-spin Balance type from Random Booster Vol. 8. The 4-80 Ratchet and Low Rush Bit offer a mix of speed and stability.

name: BulletGriffon H
assessment: BulletGriffon H is a right-spin Balance type Unique Line Starter and the first Expand Blade (Infinity) release. Its Ratchet-Integrated Blade splits into an upper Bullet piece and lower Griffon body during battle, using the Hexa Bit.

name: GloryValkyrie LF
assessment: GloryValkyrie LF is a right-spin Attack type Unique Line Starter and an homage to the Valkyrie motif. Its Ratchet-Integrated Blade features a spring-loaded bound mechanism, paired with the Low Flat Bit for aggressive movement.

name: DranBrave S6-60V
assessment: DranBrave S6-60V is a right-spin Attack type Custom Line Starter. It combines the Dran Lock Chip, Brave Main Blade, Slash Assist Blade, 6-60 Ratchet and Vortex Bit for aggressive, high-speed attacks.

name: WizardArc R4-55LO
assessment: WizardArc R4-55LO is a right-spin Stamina type Custom Line Starter. It combines the Wizard Lock Chip, Arc Main Blade, Round Assist Blade, 4-55 Ratchet and Low Orb Bit for long spin times.

name: PerseusDark B6-80W
assessment: PerseusDark B6-80W is a right-spin Defense type Custom Line Booster. It combines the Perseus Lock Chip, Dark Main Blade, Bumper Assist Blade, 6-80 Ratchet and Wedge Bit for stability and counter-attacks.

name: DranBrave S6-60V (Special Ver.)
assessment: DranBrave S6-60V (Special Ver.) is the color variant included in the Battle Entry Set C, using the same parts as the CX-01 release.

name: PerseusDark B6-80W (Special Ver.)
assessment: PerseusDark B6-80W (Special Ver.) is the color variant included in the Battle Entry Set C, using the same parts as the CX-03 release.

name: HellsReaper T4-70K
assessment: HellsReaper T4-70K is a right-spin Balance type and one of two prize Beys in Random Booster Vol. 6. It combines the Hells Lock Chip, Reaper Main Blade, Turn Assist Blade, 4-70 Ratchet and Kick Bit.

name: RhinoReaper C4-55D
assessment: RhinoReaper C4-55D is a right-spin Defense type and one of two prize Beys in Random Booster Vol. 6. It combines the Rhino Lock Chip, Reaper Main Blade, Charge Assist Blade, 4-55 Ratchet and Dot Bit.

name: HellsArc T3-85O
assessment: HellsArc T3-85O is a right-spin Stamina type from Random Booster Vol. 6. It combines the Hells Lock Chip, Arc Main Blade, Turn Assist Blade, 3-85 Ratchet and Orb Bit.

name: LeonCrest 9-80K
assessment: LeonCrest 9-80K is a right-spin Defense type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 6, pairing the LeonCrest Blade with the 9-80 Ratchet and Kick Bit.

name: PhoenixRudder 4-70LF
assessment: PhoenixRudder 4-70LF is a right-spin Stamina type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 6, using the 4-70 Ratchet and Low Flat Bit.

name: WhaleWave 7-60K
assessment: WhaleWave 7-60K is a right-spin Balance/Stamina type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 6, pairing the WhaleWave Blade with the 7-60 Ratchet and Kick Bit.

name: FoxBrush J9-70GR
assessment: FoxBrush J9-70GR is a right-spin Attack type and the prize Bey of Random Booster FoxBrush Select. It combines the Fox Lock Chip, Brush Main Blade, Jaggy Assist Blade, 9-70 Ratchet and Gear Rush Bit.

name: FoxBrush J0-80DB
assessment: FoxBrush J0-80DB is a right-spin Stamina type variant from Random Booster FoxBrush Select, using the 0-80 Ratchet and Disk Ball Bit.

name: FoxBrush J2-60U
assessment: FoxBrush J2-60U is a right-spin Balance type variant from Random Booster FoxBrush Select, using the 2-60 Ratchet and Unite Bit.

name: PegasusBlast ATr
assessment: PegasusBlast ATr is a right-spin Attack type Custom Line Starter. It introduces the Ratchet-Integrated Turbo Bit, combining the Pegasus Lock Chip, Blast Main Blade and Assault Assist Blade for high-speed, late-game bursts.

name: CerberusFlame W5-80WB
assessment: CerberusFlame W5-80WB is a right-spin Stamina type and one of two prize Beys in Random Booster Vol. 7. It combines the Cerberus Lock Chip, Flame Main Blade, Wheel Assist Blade, 5-80 Ratchet and Wall Ball Bit.

name: WhaleFlame M3-85HT
assessment: WhaleFlame M3-85HT is a right-spin Balance type and one of two prize Beys in Random Booster Vol. 7. It combines the Whale Lock Chip, Flame Main Blade, Massive Assist Blade, 3-85 Ratchet and High Taper Bit.

name: CerberusDark W1-60F
assessment: CerberusDark W1-60F is a right-spin Attack type from Random Booster Vol. 7. It combines the Cerberus Lock Chip, Dark Main Blade, Wheel Assist Blade, 1-60 Ratchet and Flat Bit.

name: DranBuster 5-80MN
assessment: DranBuster 5-80MN is a right-spin Balance/Defense type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 7, pairing the DranBuster Blade with the 5-80 Ratchet and Metal Needle Bit.

name: BlackShell 7-70WB
assessment: BlackShell 7-70WB is a right-spin Stamina type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 7, using the 7-70 Ratchet and Wall Ball Bit.

name: CobaltDragoon 4-55WB
assessment: CobaltDragoon 4-55WB is a right-spin Stamina type re-release from Random Booster Vol. 7, pairing the CobaltDragoon Blade with the 4-55 Ratchet and Wall Ball Bit.

name: SolEclipse D5-70TK
assessment: SolEclipse D5-70TK is a right-spin Balance type Custom Line Starter. It combines the Sol Lock Chip, Eclipse Main Blade, Dual Assist Blade, 5-70 Ratchet and Trans Kick Bit, which can switch between High and Low modes.

name: WolfHunt F0-60DB
assessment: WolfHunt F0-60DB is a right-spin Stamina type Custom Line Booster. It combines the Wolf Lock Chip, Hunt Main Blade, Free Assist Blade, 0-60 Ratchet and Disk Ball Bit, succeeding SilverWolf.
